userStories.title: Define episode metadata and structure
userStories.description: Set the episode title, number, target duration, guest info (if any), and outline the segment structure with time allocations.
acceptanceCriteria: Episode title is engaging and descriptive
Total segment durations add up to target episode length
Each segment has a clear topic and purpose
Guest bio included if applicable

userStories.title: Write the cold open and hook
userStories.description: Write the first 60 seconds of the episode. This should grab attention immediately — a surprising fact, a provocative question, or a relatable story.
acceptanceCriteria: Under 60 seconds when read at conversational pace (~150 words)
Creates curiosity or stakes for the listener
Feels natural when spoken aloud, not written
Transitions smoothly into the intro

userStories.title: Write main discussion segments
userStories.description: Write the core segments with talking points, discussion questions for co-hosts, and natural transitions. Include host cues and pivot points.
acceptanceCriteria: Each segment has 3-5 discussion-ready talking points
Questions are open-ended (not yes/no)
Transitions between segments feel conversational
Includes at least one concrete example or story per segment

userStories.title: Write outro, CTA, and show notes
userStories.description: Write the closing segment with key takeaways, calls to action, next episode tease, and compile the full show notes with timestamps and links.
acceptanceCriteria: Outro summarizes episode in 2-3 sentences
At least one clear call to action
Show notes include timestamps for each segment
All mentioned resources have links

userStories.title: Revision pass: speakability and pacing
userStories.description: Read the entire script with a focus on how it sounds. Fix anything that's too formal, too dense, or doesn't flow in conversation. Adjust pacing so no segment drags.
acceptanceCriteria: No jargon without explanation
Talking points are concise enough to riff on
Segment pacing varies (not all the same length/energy)
Host handoffs are clear
